section: 4. How do we process your personal data?
text:
How long will my data be saved?

After a customer account is deactivated, the following retention periods apply:

Without product usage: 3 years
With product usage (invoicing): 14 years (in accordance with tax and commercial
law retention obligations)
Unverified customer accounts: 14 days after the end of the failed verification
period

Once these periods have expired, your data will be deleted.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
The GDPR grants you the following rights regarding your data:

Right of access:
You can request information at any time about whether and which personal data
we process about you.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
Right to rectification:
If the data we have stored about you is incorrect or incomplete, you can request
its correction or completion.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
Right to erasure ("right to be forgotten"):
You can request the deletion of your data if it is no longer required for its
original purpose.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
However, please note that immediate deletion may not be
possible in some cases due to legal or contractual retention obligations.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text: Right to restriction of processing:

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
If, for example, you dispute the accuracy of your data or believe that the
processing is unlawful, you can request that we restrict the processing of your
data.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
Right to data portability:
You have the right to receive your data in a structured, commonly used, and
machine-readable format.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
You can also request that we transfer the data directly
to a third party, if technically feasible.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
Right to object:
You can object to the processing of your personal data unless there are
compelling legitimate reasons for the processing.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
Right not to be subject to automated decision-making:
We do not make any automated decisions regarding your person.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
If we did, you
would have the right not to be evaluated solely by algorithms.

section: 5. What rights do I have when it comes to my data?
text:
If you would like to make use of any of these rights, please contact our data
protection officer directly.

section: 6. Making complaints to the relevant data protection authority
text:
Making complaints to the relevant data protection authority

If you believe that we are violating any data protection laws by processing
personal data, you also have the right to make a complaint to the data
supervisory authority who is responsible for our company.

section: 7. Voluntary nature of data provision
text:
7. Voluntary nature of data provision

There is no statutory obligation for you to provide your data to Hetzner Online
GmbH.

section: 7. Voluntary nature of data provision
text:
Your decision to provide us with your personal data is completely
voluntary.
